verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand encoded as a single-pass type I transmembrane precursor (~46 kDa) that is expressed on the cell surface before being shed by ADAM10/ADAM17 (TACE) sheddases into the mature soluble ~46-kDa glycoprotein. The TM-anchored proform supports juxtacrine signaling, but the canonical biological actor is the shed soluble ectodomain, making the TM stage transient rather than stable. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern → contextual / dual_localization.
